info.title: Agent API
info.description: Hyperweaver Agent API v1 — the shared host-agent contract (architecture D1), as implemented by hyperweaver-agent (VirtualBox/Vagrant, Go). Reference implementation: zoneweaver-agent (Bhyve/OmniOS, Node). Capabilities are advertised by the public GET /api/status (role, hypervisors, console, auth, features) and drive conditional UI rendering. This document describes only the endpoints this agent implements today and grows with it; the console group lands with the console phase. The implementing application version is `info.x-app-version`; `info.version` is the frozen contract line. Authorization is role-based (admin > operator > viewer) and enforced by a central method+path policy; each operation notes its minimum role.

tags.name: Status
tags.description: Public identity and capability discovery (Hyperweaver dual-mode contract)

tags.name: API Keys
tags.description: API-key lifecycle (Agent API v1 local auth tier)

tags.name: Local Login
tags.description: Local-presence sign-in: the tray Open handoff and the hwa:// protocol handler

tags.name: Task Management
tags.description: The task queue: every machine operation runs as a prioritized, dependency-chained task with streamed output

tags.name: Machine Management
tags.description: VirtualBox machines, built and provisioned natively by the agent (the zoneweaver mechanism: create orchestration → SSH/ansible pipeline; vagrant is never executed — externally-created vagrant projects are still discovered read-only), operated through queued tasks

tags.name: Console
tags.description: Machine consoles: SSH terminal sessions (interactive shell over the provisioning transport), the VNC websockify bridge onto the machine's VRDE port (requires a usable VBoxVNC extpack — the console list in /api/status advertises vnc only then), no-session framebuffer screenshots, and the WebSocket ticket every upgrade requires. WebSocket upgrades authenticate by ?ticket= (minted at GET /ws-ticket), never by API-key headers — browser WebSocket clients cannot send them.

tags.name: Provisioning
tags.description: Provisioner package registry (the provisioner-registry capability token — finer than the cross-agent provisioning token, which any provisioning-capable agent advertises regardless of wire shape): packages in SHI's on-disk format — <name>/provisioner-collection.yml with <version>/provisioner.yml trees — listed, imported (folder, archive, or git clone; task-queued), and deleted only while no machine references them. One system for every package family; installer-bundled packages seed on startup without ever overwriting existing versions. Hosts.template.yml renders with TRUE Jinja2 semantics (gonja): parenthesized filter args, loop.*, is defined; undefined variables render empty; includes resolve inside the package's templates/ dir only.

tags.name: Artifacts
tags.description: Installer file cache (the artifacts capability token, config-gated by assets.enabled — SHI's file cache with hash verification implemented in FULL, Mark's 2026-07-06 ruling): SHA-256-hashed installer/fixpack/hotfix files under <cache>/<role>/{installers,fixpacks,hotfixes}/, with hash EXPECTATIONS (bundled registry seed + the HCL catalog's authoritative hashes), on-demand scans, verified URL/HCL-portal downloads with live progress, streaming uploads, and local-path registration. Machine prepare REFUSES any referenced file that is absent, unhashed, or hash-mismatched; mounts are hard links or hash-verified copies.

tags.name: Swap Management
tags.description: Read-only swap information (the swap capability token); add/remove are OmniOS semantics and not implemented on this agent

tags.name: Host Monitoring
tags.description: Host telemetry (the monitoring capability token): always-on realtime sampling via gopsutil; monitoring.storage_enabled adds stored time series in per-datatype database files. Illumos-only families (ZFS pools/datasets/ARC, zpool-iostat disk IO, netstat routes) have no analog on this host and are absent.

tags.name: Processes
tags.description: Host process listing and control (the processes capability token). The zone concept does not exist here: the zone field is absent and ?zone= filters are ignored. Absent by platform: /{pid}/stack, /{pid}/limits (pstack/plimit are illumos tools) and trace/start (DTrace).

tags.name: System Host Management
tags.description: Host power management (the host-power capability token, config-gated by host_power.enabled — every endpoint answers 503 and the token disappears from /api/status when disabled). Absent by platform: runlevels, single-user/multi-user transitions, fast reboot, reboot-required tracking (init semantics with no cross-platform analog).

tags.name: Host Configuration
tags.description: System hosts-file control on all three platforms (Windows System32\drivers\etc\hosts, /etc/hosts elsewhere). /system/dns is absent by platform: resolv.conf is Unix-only and Windows/macOS manage DNS elsewhere.

tags.name: Database Management
tags.description: SQLite statistics and maintenance across every open database file (tasks.sqlite, agent.sqlite, and the monitoring files when storage is enabled)

tags.name: Settings
tags.description: Agent configuration over the API (admin only)

tags.name: Secrets
tags.description: Global secrets store (the secrets capability token; admin only): SHI's six categories in secrets.yaml beside the config — kept OUT of /settings so that surface stays a pure configuration document. Values are plain text by design (it is the user's local machine; the generated Hosts.yml carries them as SECRETS_* template vars). Independently, Hosts.rb merges the working copy's secrets.yml/.secrets.yml at vagrant runtime — both mechanisms coexist.

ApiKeyAuth.description: API key in Bearer format: `Authorization: Bearer hw_your_api_key_here`

XApiKeyAuth.name: X-API-Key
XApiKeyAuth.description: API key in the X-API-Key header (equivalent to the Bearer form)

Status.description: Public identity + capabilities document (drives dual-mode UI rendering)

console.description: ssh always (SSH terminal sessions); vnc only when a USABLE VBoxVNC VRDE module is installed (the websockify bridge needs RFB on the VRDE port)

shi_mode.description: "I Can't Believe it's not Super.Human.Installer" presentation toggle (ui.shi_mode): the SPA renders the opinionated SHI-style theme/flow in Direct mode when true. Absent/false on agents without the concept.

features.description: Capability tokens (platform support ∧ config state): host-power is absent when host_power.enabled is false, artifacts when assets.enabled is false. provisioner-registry names the SHI-format package registry surface and secrets the global secrets store — finer than the cross-agent provisioning/machine-create tokens, which are true on every provisioning-capable agent regardless of wire shape (Mark's gating ruling, 2026-07-06). machine-modify names PUT's infrastructure-modify surface (the base's zone_modify contract).

description.example: API key for the Hyperweaver Server

role.description: Authorization role of the key (Agent API v1 role model)

Tool.description: One detected prerequisite (SHI parity: presence + version)

Task.description: One task-queue entry (Agent API v1 Task schema).

operation.description: Operation kind. Vocabulary: start, stop, suspend, delete, discover, machine_modify (PUT's infrastructure changes), restart (never dispatched — a restart is a stop→start chain); create orchestration: machine_create_orchestration (parent), machine_prepare, machine_create_storage, machine_create_config, machine_create_finalize, template_download; provisioning pipeline: machine_provision_orchestration (parent), machine_wait_ssh, machine_sync_parent, machine_sync (one per folder), machine_provision_parent, machine_provision (one per playbook); network: provisioning_network_setup, provisioning_network_teardown; consoles/snapshots: reset, pause, resume, snapshot_take, snapshot_restore, snapshot_delete, machine_clone_current (clonevm current-state clone); templates: template_download, template_delete, template_export, template_upload (publish), template_move; registry/cache/system: provisioner_import, artifact_scan, artifact_download, hcl_download, agent_update.

status.description: completed_with_errors is a parent-task terminal state: some children failed, some completed

priority.description: Higher runs first: 100 critical, 80 high, 60 medium, 50 service, 40 low, 20 background

created_by.description: API key name (or system source) that created the task

depends_on.description: Predecessor that must complete first; its failure or cancellation cancels this task

parent_task_id.description: Progress-aggregation anchor this task reports into

updatedAt.description: Last modification — the since query parameter compares against this

progress_info.description: Freeform progress detail; parent tasks carry {completed_tasks, failed_tasks, total_tasks, status}

output.description: JSON-encoded array of output entries as last flushed — ALWAYS null on GET /tasks (the list never carries output blobs); GET /tasks/{taskId} (detail), GET /tasks/{taskId}/output, and the /tasks/{taskId}/stream WebSocket serve it in full

Machine.description: One machine registry row (Agent API v1 Machine schema; backing/home are this agent's dual-path fields). Machine names are free user-chosen identifiers (design D-G).

status.description: VirtualBox is authoritative; configured means a registry row with no VM behind it yet

backing.description: Provenance metadata: vagrant means a vagrant project (home) owns this VM — used by provisioning operations. Lifecycle always drives VBoxManage directly.

server_id.description: Numeric server identifier (assigned at creation; see /machines/ids)

is_orphaned.description: In the registry but no longer present in VirtualBox

auto_discovered.description: Imported by the reconciliation sweep rather than created through the agent

configuration.description: Live machine configuration (VBoxManage showvminfo --machinereadable key/value map on this agent)

spec.description: The creation document (machines this agent created; discovered VMs have none) — the user's intent, never overwritten by discovery

MachineSpec.description: The machine-create document (the Hosts.yml-shaped structure, design §4), stored verbatim on the machine row. The provisioner reference is OPTIONAL — a machine is just a machine, provisioning is optional (the base's provisioner-free create). WITH a provisioner: machine_prepare renders the package's Jinja2 templates/Hosts.template.yml — at create and again before every provision — and the rendered hosts[0] drives everything: its settings/networks/disks build the VM natively (VBoxManage), and its folders/provisioning/vars/roles become the stored provisioner document the pipeline consumes. WITHOUT one: no prepare/render step exists, the spec's own settings/networks build the VM directly, finalize persists no provisioner, and a provisioner document can attach later via PUT /machines/{name} + POST /provision. Template context precedence (package-based creates): settings (also flattened UPPERCASE) → per-role <ROLE>_INSTALLER/_INSTALLER_HASH/_INSTALLER_VERSION/_FIXPACK*/_HOTFIX* vars + boolean role flags (both casings) → package configuration.basicFields/advancedFields defaults → properties/advanced_properties (exact field names) → SECRETS_* vars. Structured settings/networks/roles ride alongside.

provisioner.description: OPTIONAL package reference. Omit it entirely for a provisioner-less machine; when present, both name and version are required and must exist in the registry.

settings.description: Hosts.yml settings vocabulary. hostname and domain are REQUIRED (machine_domain optionally overrides the naming domain); with machines.prefix_machine_names server_id is REQUIRED too (numeric 1-8 digits, padded to 4 — never auto-assigned; GET /machines/ids/next prefills it). box/box_version/box_arch/box_url select the template (package defaults apply); vcpus/memory/os_type/firmware_type/consoleport/consolehost/setup_wait/vagrant_user* drive the build and the pipeline. boot_order: ordered list of floppy|dvd|disk|net|none → --boot1..4 (remaining slots cleared) — the ISO-first install story with disks.cdroms.

disks.description: OPTIONAL disks section at create (the base's contract — omit entirely for a DISKLESS stub; attach media later via modify). boot: {path: attach an existing disk-image file} | {size, sparse?, volume_name?: create a blank scratch volume} | omitted with settings.box: template clone | omitted without box: no boot medium. additional_disks[]: {volume_name, size, sparse?, port?} to create, or {path, port?} to attach existing. cdroms[]: [{path: agent-host ISO path}] — the attach-an-ISO-and-install-yourself flow. With a provisioner package the render's disks win.

zones.description: OPTIONAL zones section at create — the base's named system fields, translated exactly like modify: autostart (host-startup), bootrom (efi|bios → firmware), hostbridge (i440fx→piix3 → chipset), vnc (on/off → VRDE; settings.consoleport sets the port), acpi, xhci, netif (virtio|e1000 → every document adapter's hardware type; the reserved NAT adapter keeps the default). diskif selects the STORAGE CONTROLLER type at create: ide|sata(default)|scsi|sas|nvme|virtio — VirtualBox fixes it once media attach, so modify refuses it.

cloud_init.description: OPTIONAL cloud-init section at create (enabled/dns_domain/password/resolvers/sshkey — set as guest properties under /Hyperweaver/CloudInit/)

vbox.description: OPTIONAL vbox section at create: directives[] = [{directive, value}] — the generic modifyvm passthrough (any --flag=value: acpi, chipset, usb-xhci, nic hardware types...), the base's zone attr-map analog

notes.description: Free-form notes, persisted onto the machine row at finalize (the base's create contract)

files.description: Installer assignments, resolved against the file cache at every prepare (assets.enabled): the named file must exist in the cache and pass SHA-256 verification or the start FAILS; hashes are auto-filled from the cache into the template vars, and a spec-supplied hash must match the cached one. Files mount into the working copy's installers/<role>/ tree as hard links or hash-verified copies.

properties.description: User entries for the package's configuration.basicFields, keyed by each field's exact name

advanced_properties.description: User entries for the package's configuration.advancedFields

sync_method.description: Per-machine file-sync preference (SHI's Rsync/SCP setting), default rsync. Platform rules apply at render time: forced rsync on Windows hosts; macOS auto-falls back to SCP when the system rsync is the ancient Apple 2.x build. The EFFECTIVE value reaches the template context as settings.sync_method / SYNC_METHOD; the stored spec keeps the preference.

safe_id_path.description: Agent-host path of a Domino safe-ID file, placed under id-files per the package's id_files metadata (safe_id_dir, keep_original_name)

start_after_create.description: POST /machines only: queue the first start immediately

ServerStats.description: Host statistics in Node's os-module vocabulary (the shared v1 stats shape) plus the machine name lists.

cpus.description: One entry per logical CPU, Node os.cpus() shape; times are cumulative milliseconds — consumers diff between polls for usage

uptime.description: Host uptime in seconds (not process uptime)

allmachines.description: All registered machine names (VBoxManage list vms)

ProvisionerVersion.description: One provisioner package version. metadata is the full provisioner.yml — metadata.roles and configuration.basicFields/advancedFields drive the machine-create forms exactly as in SHI's custom-provisioner stack.

version.description: The manifest's version field (falls back to the directory name)

dir.description: Version directory name — the URL segment

root.description: Absolute path of the version directory on the agent host

ProvisionerCollection.description: One provisioner family. name is the registry directory name — the stable identity URLs address.

valid.description: false marks a family with no parseable versions (SHI's invalid placeholder) — still listed, still deletable

Artifact.description: One file-cache row. sha256 is what the file actually hashed to when last verified; expected_sha256 is the recorded expectation (bundled registry seed, caller-supplied, or the HCL catalog). exists:false rows are expectations awaiting their binary (SHI's model).

SecretsDocument.description: The whole secrets store — SHI's six category names, verbatim. Every entry name must match [a-zA-Z0-9_-]+. SECRETS_* template-var derivation: name→uppercase, hyphens→underscores; key categories become SECRETS_<NAME>, custom_resource_url becomes SECRETS_<NAME>_URL (+_USER/_PASS when useAuth), docker_hub becomes SECRETS_<NAME>_USER/_TOKEN, ssh_keys becomes SECRETS_<NAME>_SSH.

git_api_keys.description: Also referenced by provisioner git imports via token_name

ProvisioningProfile.description: One reusable provisioning profile (the base's model, minus the zlogin-only recipe_id): the UI composes provisioning without a bundled Hosts.yml or provisioner package and applies it into a machine's provisioner document.

MonitoringSampling.description: Time-series sampling metadata. This agent never downsamples (applied is always false); strategy names which mode answered — realtime (single live sample; storage disabled) or stored (database rows).

CPUSample.description: One CPU telemetry observation (the shared CPUStats shape's platform-feasible subset — illumos-only counters are absent). Utilization percentages are deltas against the previous observation.

load_avg_1min.description: Zeros on Windows — the platform has no load averages

processes_running.description: Run-queue count where the platform reports one (Linux); zero elsewhere

MemorySample.description: One memory + swap telemetry observation (the shared MemoryStats shape's platform-feasible subset — ZFS ARC fields are absent).

NetworkSample.description: One interface's telemetry observation (the shared NetworkUsage shape's platform-feasible subset): cumulative OS counters plus rates computed from the previous observation.

get.description: Canonical path of the public status probe. No authentication.

get.summary: Public identity and capabilities (SPA discovery alias)
get.description: Unconditional alias of GET /status — the single mode-discovery probe URL for the Hyperweaver UI. No authentication.

Minimum role: viewer — unless the agent is configured with `stats.public_access: true`, which serves this endpoint without an API key (the Node agent's conditional /stats registration). OS-level host statistics plus the registered and running VirtualBox machine name lists. Machine-list failures degrade to empty arrays — a broken VBoxManage never fails the host stats.

post.description: Public, but gated: requires `api_keys.bootstrap_enabled`, locks after the first key when `bootstrap_auto_disable` is on, and (by default) demands the setup claim token written to `setup.token` beside the agent's config file and printed to the startup log — proof of host ownership. The first key is always an admin key.

setupToken.description: 64-character claim token from the agent host

200.description: Key created — shown once, only a hash is stored

note.example: Bootstrap endpoint will be auto-disabled for future requests

403.description: Bootstrap disabled, auto-disabled, or setup token invalid

post.summary: Exchange a tray one-time token for an admin API key
post.description: Public: the token itself is the credential — minted seconds earlier by the local user's physical tray Open click (or an hwa:// protocol invocation), carried in the URL fragment, single-use, 60-second TTL. This is how a desktop user gets a signed-in UI without ever seeing a login screen.

token.description: Single-use tray token from the #tray= URL fragment

200.description: Fresh admin key, named after the local OS account

post.description: Public but secret-gated: when the OS spawns a fresh agent process for an hwa://open invocation (Windows registry handler, Linux .desktop handler), that process forwards the action here and exits. The per-boot secret file (0600, beside the running agent's config) authenticates it — web pages cannot read local files, so possession proves a local same-user process. On success the running agent opens the signed-in UI in the user's browser, exactly like a tray Open click.

secret.description: Contents of the running agent's protocol.secret file

200.description: Action accepted; the agent is opening the browser

post.description: Minimum role: admin.

description.example: API key for the Hyperweaver Server

200.description: Key created — shown once, only a hash is stored

get.description: Minimum role: admin. Hashes are never returned.
Minimum role: viewer (every valid key may inspect itself).

delete.description: Minimum role: admin. Deleting the last active admin key is refused (lockout guard).

409.description: Would remove the last active admin key

put.description: Minimum role: admin. Revoking the last active admin key is refused (lockout guard).

409.description: Would deactivate the last active admin key

get.description: Minimum role: viewer. Includes the detected provisioning prerequisites (Vagrant, VirtualBox, Git, rsync, scp, plus builtin_sync — the embedded pure-Go transports) and host information — SHI's footer data.
Minimum role: viewer. Fetches the configured versioninfo document (updates.versioninfo_url) and compares against the running build.

post.summary: Download and apply an agent update
post.description: Minimum role: admin. Queues an agent_update task (SHI's download-then-relaunch, Mark's ruling 2026-07-06): downloads this platform's installer from the release named by updates.versioninfo_url, verifies it against the release's SHA256SUMS.txt (MANDATORY — an unverifiable installer never launches), starts it (the .exe directly on Windows, open for the macOS .pkg, xdg-open for the Linux .deb — headless Linux keeps the download and reports the dpkg command), and exits the agent so the installer can replace it.

400.description: Update checking not configured, or already up to date

get.description: Minimum role: viewer. A bare tool-name → installed map; this agent's tools are vagrant, virtualbox, git, rsync, scp, and builtin_sync (always true — the embedded pure-Go rsync client + SFTP that make vagrant optional). rsync/scp probe with the pipeline's own lookup: PATH, vagrant's embedded toolchain, and the Windows OpenSSH directory. (The Node agent lists its OmniOS set — same endpoint, platform-appropriate contents.)
Minimum role: viewer. Filterable and sortable; the total count is computed only when include_count=true.

parameters.name: since
parameters.description: Only tasks modified (updatedAt) at or after this time — incremental refresh

parameters.name: include_count
parameters.description: Also return the total matching count

get.description: Minimum role: viewer.
Minimum role: viewer.

delete.description: Minimum role: operator. Pending tasks are cancelled immediately. Running tasks are cancellable too (this agent's D-F extension over the Node reference): the executor's child processes are killed, its per-operation cleanup runs, and the task lands in cancelled with its output preserved. Tasks pending on a cancelled task are cancelled by dependency propagation, and cancelling a parent orchestration anchor cascades to its whole chain — pending children flip, running children get their contexts killed.

400.description: Task already in a terminal state

error.example: Can only cancel pending or running tasks

get.description: Minimum role: viewer. The live in-memory buffer while the task runs; the persisted output afterwards.

delete.description: Minimum role: operator. Hard-deletes every completed, completed_with_errors, failed, and cancelled task immediately. Pending and running tasks are untouched.

post.description: Minimum role: operator (the machine-create capability token). Queues the CREATE ORCHESTRATION (the zoneweaver mechanism — the agent builds real infrastructure natively, no vagrant): a parent task plus chained children machine_prepare (render the package's Jinja2 template + materialize the working directory + hash-verified installer mounts — queued ONLY when the spec names a provisioner; the reference is OPTIONAL, a machine is just a machine and provisioning never gates its existence) → machine_create_storage (clone the box template's disk image, grow to disks.boot.size, create additional media; rollback on failure) → machine_create_config (createvm/modifyvm/storage/NICs from the document + vbox.directives passthrough + cloud-init guest properties; adapter 1 is the PROVISIONING NIC — NAT with an allocated ssh port-forward (127.0.0.1:<port> → guest 22), giving guest egress and the host's provisioning access, vagrant's exact layout; document networks[] ride adapters 2+) → machine_create_finalize (registry row + document sections + — package-based creates only — the render-produced provisioner document stored in configuration + UUID) → optional start. Provisioner-less creates build straight from the spec's own settings/networks; a provisioner document can attach later via PUT + POST /provision (the base's model). The machine ROW appears at finalize, not at POST. settings.hostname AND settings.domain are REQUIRED (settings.machine_domain overrides the naming domain). With machines.prefix_machine_names, settings.server_id is REQUIRED (numeric, padded to 4; never auto-assigned — GET /machines/ids/next feeds the field) and the name becomes <id>--<hostname>.<domain>. settings.box is OPTIONAL (the base's contract): present, it resolves against the local template registry and a missing box chains a template_download child in front (requires_download: true; box_version must then be specific); absent, the boot medium comes from disks.boot (existing path | scratch size) or the machine is DISKLESS — a stub, Proxmox-style: hostname + domain alone makes a machine, media/NICs attach later via modify, first start is manual. notes/tags/cloud_init/zones/vbox.directives are accepted at create too.

name.description: Free user-chosen machine name (design D-G) — anything goes; omit it and the name derives from the spec as described above.

200.description: Create orchestration queued (the machine row lands at the finalize child)

parent_task_id.description: The orchestration parent — poll it for overall progress

requires_download.description: Whether a template_download child was chained in front

resource_warnings.description: Present when resource validation passed with threshold warnings (machines.resource_validation)

400.description: Invalid spec, provisioner reference, server_id, safe_id_path, box reference, template render failure, or Insufficient resources ({error, details[]} — the pre-flight resource validation rejection, machines.resource_validation)

409.description: Machine name (DB or hypervisor), server_id, or working directory already in use

get.description: Minimum role: viewer. Machines built outside the agent appear here once the reconciliation sweep imports them.

Minimum role: viewer. Used server_ids, constraints, and the next available id — create NEVER auto-assigns (with prefix_machine_names the caller must send settings.server_id; this endpoint and /machines/ids/next feed the field).
Minimum role: viewer.
Minimum role: viewer. Live-checks VirtualBox (updating the registry on drift) and returns the record, its live configuration, and pending tasks.

configuration.description: The stored document sections (settings/zones/networks/disks/metadata) plus provisioner (the Hosts.yml host-entry document PUT stores and the render auto-fills) and provisioner_state ({last_provisioned_at} — drives the playbooks' run directives), MERGED with the live VirtualBox machinereadable view. Discovery refreshes the live half and never clobbers the stored sections.

system_status.description: Live VirtualBox state; the stored status for created-but-never-started machines

web_address.description: Post-provision welcome URL read from the working copy's results.yml — null unless something writes one (the native pipeline does not; the document's control IP is authoritative addressing)

put.description: Minimum role: operator (the machine-modify capability token). The zoneweaver modify mechanism, changed-fields-only body with three change classes. (1) notes/tags: immediate registry-only updates. (2) provisioner: the PROVISIONER DOCUMENT stored verbatim into configuration.provisioner — a Hosts.yml host entry (folders[], provisioning.ansible.playbooks in object or list form, vars, roles[], secrets, pre_tasks/post_tasks, ssh_port); an immediate DB-only update the next /provision consumes; create's finalize auto-fills it from the rendered package template and PUT overrides it whole. (3) every infrastructure field queues a machine_modify task and the answer carries requires_restart: true — VirtualBox applies configuration only to a POWERED-OFF machine, so the task fails with guidance while the machine runs; stop it and re-run. Translations from the base vocabulary: ram→memory, vcpus→cpus, os_type→ostype, vnc→VRDE on/off, acpi/xhci→--acpi/--usb-xhci, autoboot→autostart, bootrom→firmware (efi when the value names EFI, else bios), hostbridge→chipset (i440fx→piix3), netif (virtio|e1000)→every configured adapter's hardware type. diskif and complex CPU topology have no modifyvm analog — reported as skipped in task output, never silently accepted. add_nics land on the first free adapters (bridged; global_nic = the bridge interface, mac_addr honored; vlan_id/allowed_address have no analog and are noted); remove_nics name adapter numbers 1-8 — on agent-created machines adapter 1 is the reserved NAT (egress) and document networks sit on 2+, so removing adapter 1 cuts the guest's internet. add_disks create media under the machine's working directory (volume_name/size/sparse) or attach an existing file by path, at the next free SATA port; add_cdroms attach ISOs by path. remove_disks/remove_cdroms name SATA PORT numbers 1 and up — port 0 is the boot medium and is refused; detached files are PRESERVED (the base never destroys the volume). cloud_init keys update the machine's guest properties. When only DB-immediate fields changed, the answer is status completed with requires_restart: false and no task.

schema.description: Changed fields only — at least one of the modify vocabulary

ram.description: Memory allocation (G/M suffixes; bare numbers are MB)

bootrom.description: Firmware selector — a value naming EFI/UEFI sets --firmware efi, anything else bios

diskif.description: Storage controller type is fixed once media attach — accepted, reported as skipped (set it at CREATE via zones.diskif)

boot_order.description: Ordered boot devices → --boot1..4; remaining slots cleared

vbox.description: The generic modifyvm passthrough at MODIFY too (same shape as create): {directives: [{directive, value}]} — any --flag=value

netif.description: Adapter hardware type for every configured NIC

global_nic.description: Bridge interface for the new adapter

path.description: Existing medium to attach instead of creating one

remove_disks.description: SATA port numbers (1+; port 0 is the boot medium and refused). Files are preserved.

provisioner.description: The Hosts.yml host-entry document, stored verbatim (DB-immediate)

boot_priority.description: Orchestration boot priority (stored in the spec's settings, DB-immediate — VirtualBox never sees it; the base's zonecfg attr analog)

200.description: Modification queued (infrastructure fields) or applied (DB-immediate fields only)

task_id.description: Present when a machine_modify task was queued

requires_restart.description: true when infrastructure changes were queued — they apply to a powered-off machine and take effect on next boot

resource_warnings.description: Present when resource validation passed with threshold warnings

400.description: No modification fields specified, an invalid field value, or Insufficient resources ({error, details[]} — the resource validation rejection)

delete.description: Minimum role: operator. Running machines need force=true, which chains a CRITICAL stop before the CRITICAL delete. The machine is powered off if still running, unregistered from VirtualBox — with media deletion when cleanup_disks (default true) — its working directory removed (only when it sits under the machines root), its registry row removed, and its leftover pending tasks cancelled. SAFETY: media whose file lives OUTSIDE the machine's working directory (user-attached disk images, external ISOs) are ALWAYS detached and preserved regardless of cleanup_disks. cleanup_disks=false unregisters only — every medium file and the working directory stay on disk.

parameters.name: cleanup_disks
parameters.description: false preserves every medium file and the working directory (the base's keep-datasets default, as an explicit flag)

400.description: Machine is running and force is not set

get.description: Minimum role: viewer. The live configuration document (VBoxManage showvminfo --machinereadable view on this agent), falling back to the last reconciled copy.

post.description: Minimum role: operator. One native start task for EVERY machine (VBoxManage startvm --type headless) — the provision pipeline queues this same operation as its boot child. Idempotent: already-running machines answer already_running; an existing pending/running start task is reused. With machines.provision_on_start enabled, a machine's VERY FIRST start (stored provisioner document, never provisioned) queues the full provision pipeline instead — the answer carries operation machine_provision_orchestration and the parent task id; later starts, restarts, and document-less machines always boot plainly.

200.description: Start task queued (or machine already running)

post.description: Minimum role: operator. Queues a stop task and cancels pending starts: ACPI power button (graceful), falling back to hard poweroff when the guest ignores it — or straight poweroff with force=true.

200.description: Stop task queued (or machine already stopped)

post.description: Minimum role: operator. Queues a HIGH-priority stop and a MEDIUM-priority start chained on it.
Minimum role: operator. Saves the machine's state (VBoxManage controlvm savestate). Advertised by the machine-suspend capability token — the UI shows Suspend only on agents that carry it.

Minimum role: operator. Orchestrates the full pipeline against the STORED provisioner document (the zoneweaver mechanism): machine_prepare (re-render + refresh the working copy — regenerated before every provision) → boot (the plain start operation as a child, skipped when running or skip_boot) → machine_wait_ssh (credentials = settings.vagrant_user/vagrant_user_pass/vagrant_user_private_key_path, agent provisioning key as last-resort fallback; transport = the provisioning NIC's NAT ssh port-forward at 127.0.0.1 when the machine carries one — vagrant's model, immune to guest network reconfiguration — else the control IP from networks[] is_control → provisional → first; neither existing is a 400) → machine_sync_parent + one machine_sync per folders[] entry (transport per folder.type: rsync | scp, each with a pure-Go fallback when the binary is absent — embedded rsync client / SFTP, loudly narrated in the task output; vagrant is optional; virtualbox/disabled skipped) → machine_provision_parent + one machine_provision per playbook, RUN-FILTERED first (always = every run; not_first = only after a prior success; once/unset = only when never provisioned — judged by configuration.provisioner_state.last_provisioned_at, stamped only when the run's FINAL playbook succeeds: a partial run never marks the machine provisioned, Hosts.rb's results.yml semantics). The extra_vars networks[] carry each adapter's LIVE MAC when the document leaves mac auto/empty (adapter = network index + 2; resolved into the run's variable document only — the stored document is never modified). Prerequisites: provisioner config stored (create auto-fills it; PUT overrides) and a control IP in networks[].

400.description: No provisioner config stored, missing settings.vagrant_user, or no control IP in networks[]

get.description: Minimum role: viewer. Whether provisioner config is stored, whether a provision ever succeeded, and the most recent provisioning tasks.

post.description: Minimum role: operator. Runs the stored document's playbooks (run-filtered) without the rest of the pipeline — the machine must be running with SSH reachable. All playbooks skipped by their run directives answers a 200 no-op carrying the skipped list; no playbooks configured at all is a 400.

200.description: Provisioner tasks created (or the all-skipped no-op)

400.description: No provisioner config, no playbooks configured, missing credentials, or no control IP

post.summary: Sync folders to a machine ad-hoc
post.description: Minimum role: operator. Creates the parentless sync chain: one machine_sync per folders[] entry from the stored document (transport per folder.type), independent of the full pipeline — the machine must be running with SSH reachable.

400.description: No provisioner config or no folders configured

get.description: Minimum role: viewer. The local box-template registry: downloaded box disk images machines clone from (organization/box_name/version/architecture tuples; stale rows whose disk image vanished self-delete on resolution).

post.description: Minimum role: operator. Queues a template_download task: the .box streams from the named source (or the default registry), its disk image lands in the template storage, and the registry row is created. Private-box credentials live on the configured source (template_sources.sources[].auth_token) — never in task metadata.

source_name.description: A configured template source; empty = the default registry

version.description: A SPECIFIC version — latest is refused

400.description: Missing tuple fields, non-specific version, or no usable source

get.description: Minimum role: viewer. The enabled Vagrant/BoxVault-compatible registries (name, url, default) — credentials are never returned. The base's GET /templates/sources.

get.summary: List a source's remote box catalog
get.description: Minimum role: viewer. Proxies the registry's discovery endpoint (BoxVault's /api/discover): every available box with its versions, providers, and architectures — the machine wizard's box-picker feed. An x-registry-token request header overrides the source's configured auth token (never returned). The base's GET /templates/remote/{sourceName}.

200.description: The registry's catalog document, relayed verbatim

502.description: Remote source unreachable or answered an error

get.description: Minimum role: viewer. Proxies the registry's Vagrant-compatible /{org}/{box} metadata document: versions with providers and download URLs. The base's GET /templates/remote/{sourceName}/{org}/{boxName}.

200.description: The box's metadata document, relayed verbatim

404.description: Source disabled, or box not on the remote

502.description: Remote source unreachable or answered an error

post.description: Minimum role: operator. Spec-carrying machines only. TWO disk semantics via source: "template" (default) feeds a SPEC copy through the SAME create orchestration as POST /machines — a fresh build from the original template (response shape identical to create, plus source_machine); "current" runs ONE machine_clone_current task: VBoxManage clonevm copies the source's CURRENT disk state (the base's ZFS-snapshot clone semantics), the clone gets a fresh provisioning ssh port-forward and VRDE off, MACs reinitialize, and the row lands with the identity-stripped spec — the source must be stopped unless snapshot names a source snapshot to clone from (linked=true makes a differencing clone against it). settings.hostname is required (a clone must not reuse the source hostname); domain and everything else default from the source spec; overrides (memory, vcpus, …) merge into settings; consoleport and server_id never survive (prefix mode requires a fresh server_id in settings). Cloned networks lose mac/address/gateway/netmask/dns so source and clone can never collide; provisional entries draw fresh IPs from the provisioning DHCP range. Resource validation runs first (400 Insufficient resources; storage is skipped for source=current — the footprint is the source's current usage, unknowable from the spec).

name.description: The clone's machine name (free user-chosen, design D-G). Omit to derive it like create does: <server_id>--<hostname>.<domain> under machines.prefix_machine_names, else <hostname>.<domain>.

settings.description: Settings entries replacing the source's (hostname required; with prefix_machine_names a fresh server_id is required too — the source's never survives)

overrides.description: Resource overrides merged into settings (zoneweaver shape)

source.description: template = spec rebuild from the original template; current = clonevm of today's disk state

snapshot.description: source=current only: clone from this source snapshot (required for linked, and for cloning a RUNNING source)

linked.description: source=current only: differencing clone against snapshot instead of a full copy

200.description: Clone create orchestration queued (the row lands at the finalize child); source=current answers {task_id, operation: machine_clone_current, ...} (+start_task_id) instead

400.description: Missing settings.hostname, invalid name, source has no creation spec, or the source's provisioner/safe-ID/box no longer resolves

409.description: Clone name, server_id, or working directory already in use

get.description: Minimum role: viewer.

put.description: Minimum role: operator. Registry-only, no task.

get.description: Minimum role: viewer.

put.description: Minimum role: operator. Registry-only, no task.

post.description: Minimum role: operator. machines is "all" (every stopped, non-orphaned machine) or a name array; already-running machines are skipped with a reason.

machines.description: "all" or an array of machine names

post.description: Minimum role: operator. machines is "all" (every running, non-orphaned machine) or a name array; already-stopped machines are skipped with a reason. Pending starts for targeted machines are cancelled.

machines.description: "all" or an array of machine names

get.description: Minimum role: viewer. Every cache row — present files and expectation-only entries. 503 when assets.enabled is false.

post.description: Minimum role: operator. Queues an artifact_scan task: new files hashed and registered, vanished files marked missing (expectations survive), and with verify_checksums every present file re-hashed — the task FAILS when any file mismatches its expectation.

post.summary: Download a file into the cache
post.description: Minimum role: operator. Queues an artifact_download task: streamed with live progress ({downloaded_mb, total_mb, speed_mbps, eta_seconds} in progress_info), hashed DURING the stream, verified against expected_sha256 when given (mismatch discards the file — never promoted, no auto-retry). resource_name names a custom_resource_url secret whose Basic-auth pair authenticates the fetch.

resource_name.description: custom_resource_url secret for HTTP Basic auth

post.summary: Download a file from the HCL portal
post.description: Minimum role: operator. Queues an hcl_download task (SHI's HCLDownloader flow): exchanges the named hcl_download_portal_api_keys secret's refresh token (the ROTATED token is persisted back immediately — SHI's critical rule), looks the file up in the HCL catalog by EXACT name (the catalog's sha256 is authoritative and overwrites the expectation), streams the verified download into the cache. Failures carry contextual guidance (expired key vs un-accepted HCL license); no auto-retry.

post.summary: Upload a file into the cache
post.description: Minimum role: operator. Streaming multipart/form-data: fields role + kind (+ optional filename) MUST precede the "file" part (single-pass stream). The upload is hashed on arrival; the response's verified flag reports the expectation check. Body capped by assets.max_upload_gb.

400.description: Malformed multipart body or unusable role/kind/filename

post.summary: Register an agent-host file into the cache
post.description: Minimum role: operator. SHI's add-file picker flow for Direct-mode installs: copies (move: true moves) the named agent-host file into the cache and hashes it.

filename.description: Defaults from the path's base name

delete.description: Minimum role: operator. delete_file=true also removes the cached file (only from its canonical cache location).

get.description: Minimum role: viewer. VBoxManage list bridgedifs names — the bridge/default-NIC picker's choices; default echoes provisioning.default_network_interface.

Minimum role: viewer. The dedicated provisioning network (the zoneweaver mechanism's etherstub+dhcpd, as ONE VirtualBox host-only interface — identified by provisioning.network.host_ip, since VirtualBox assigns interface names itself — plus its DHCP server). Disabled answers just {enabled:false, message}; enabled answers ready + per-component existence + the effective configuration. The base's NAT/forwarding components translate to the create-time NAT adapter + ssh port-forward transport (the provisioning NIC); this host-only machinery stays dormant-but-available for host-type networks[] entries and build-it-yourself setups.

message.description: Present only on the disabled branch

post.description: Minimum role: operator. Queues a provisioning_network_setup task (category-locked — one network mutation at a time), idempotent at every component: the host-only interface is created only when none carries the configured host_ip, its address always converges onto the configuration, and the DHCP server (subnet range, its own dhcp_server_ip) is added or modified to match. Machines with host-type networks[] entries then attach to this interface at create, and each entry's address pins as a per-VM-NIC DHCP fixed lease — the guest's ordinary DHCP request receives the document's own control IP, so wait_ssh dials a deterministic address.

400.description: Provisioning network is disabled in configuration

delete.description: Minimum role: operator. Queues a provisioning_network_teardown task: the DHCP server first, then the host-only interface (the base's reverse order); absent components are noted, never errors.

400.description: Provisioning network is disabled in configuration

get.description: Minimum role: viewer. Reusable bundles of credentials, sync folders, provisioners, and variables — composed in the UI without bundling a Hosts.yml or provisioner package, applied by feeding the pieces into a machine's provisioner document (PUT /machines/{name}).

post.description: Minimum role: operator. name is required and unique.

get.description: Minimum role: viewer.

put.description: Minimum role: operator. Only submitted fields change.

get.description: Minimum role: viewer. Every package family in the registry with its versions (newest first). The filesystem is the source of truth — packages dropped in by installers or by hand appear without registration.

post.description: Minimum role: operator. Queues a provisioner_import task (machine_name "system", category-locked: one import at a time). Sources: folder (a directory on the agent host holding provisioner-collection.yml, or provisioner.yml for a bare version), archive (.tar.gz/.tgz/.zip on the agent host), or git (http(s) URL, cloned --depth 1 --recursive so nested submodules arrive; optional branch). The package root is searched up to 3 directory levels deep. Existing versions are never touched — re-importing is idempotent; update = import a newer version beside the old.

token_name.description: Optional git_api_keys secret name for private repositories (git source); the token itself never enters task metadata

400.description: Invalid body, source_type, path, url, or branch

get.description: Minimum role: viewer. The family with its full version metadata.

delete.description: Minimum role: operator. Removes the family and every version — refused (409) while any machine references any of its versions. No package is special-cased by name.

get.description: Minimum role: viewer. One version's full manifest — the document the machine-create forms render from. version matches the manifest version or the directory name.

delete.description: Minimum role: operator. Removes one version, leaving its siblings — refused (409) while any machine references it (versions in use are never touched).

get.description: Minimum role: viewer. Aggregate swap figures, per-area breakdown, and utilization recommendations (Node-agent shape). Pool fields are present but empty — ZFS pools have no analog on this host. Read live from the OS; lastScanned is the request time.
Minimum role: viewer. Row-per-area listing with the Node payload's pagination envelope, read live from the OS. The zvol pool filter has no meaning on this agent and is ignored.
Minimum role: viewer. Single-host agent: this host appears in the list when its live swap utilization exceeds the threshold.
Minimum role: viewer. Realtime mode (storage disabled, the default): one live sample; since is effectively ignored. Storage mode: stored samples, newest first.

parameters.name: since
parameters.description: Stored samples at or after this time (storage mode)

parameters.name: include_cores
parameters.description: Include the per_core_parsed array on every sample

get.description: Minimum role: viewer. Realtime mode: one live sample; storage mode: stored samples, newest first.
Minimum role: viewer. Load averages and process activity reshaped for charting. Load averages are zeros on Windows; run-queue counts are Linux-only — absent counters stay zero, honestly.

500.description: Failed to get system load metrics

get.description: Minimum role: viewer.
Minimum role: viewer. Record counts and latest sample times per telemetry table (empty in realtime mode — nothing is stored).
Minimum role: viewer.
Minimum role: viewer. healthy | degraded (collector errors) | stopped (storage on, collector down). No fault-management rollup — fmadm is illumos-only.

post.description: Minimum role: operator. One collection pass over every telemetry family this host has (cpu, memory, network); samples persist when storage is enabled. The type field is accepted for contract parity — there is no illumos storage family to exclude here.

get.description: Minimum role: viewer. Live configuration view (name, MTU, state, MAC, addresses). dladm-only fields (over, speed, vid, zone) have no analog and are absent.
Minimum role: viewer. Per-interface counters with computed rates. Realtime mode: one live observation per interface; storage mode: stored samples, newest first.
Minimum role: viewer. Live view derived from the interface list (the reduced live shape — this agent has no ipadm address-object database).
Minimum role: viewer. Bare array response. detailed=true adds CPU/memory statistics (process-lifetime CPU percentage — instant, no sampling wait).
Minimum role: viewer.
Minimum role: viewer. Processes ranked by CPU usage (top 30) — one instant sample.

post.description: Minimum role: operator. The agent's own process is always refused. On Windows only TERM and KILL exist.

get.description: Minimum role: viewer. Detailed statistics plus a sample of open files (empty on platforms where per-process file enumeration is unsupported).
Minimum role: viewer. Degrades to an empty list on platforms where gopsutil cannot enumerate per-process files (Windows).

post.summary: Send a signal to a process
post.description: Minimum role: operator. On Windows only TERM and KILL exist (delivered via TerminateProcess); other signals answer 400 — never a silent no-op.

400.description: Invalid process ID or unsupported signal

post.description: Minimum role: operator. Graceful terminate; force=true kills immediately.

get.description: Minimum role: viewer. Uptime, load averages, and memory. No runlevel or reboot-required tracking — init concepts absent on this platform trio. 503 when host_power.enabled is false.

503.description: Host power management is disabled in configuration

get.description: Minimum role: viewer. 503 when host_power.enabled is false.

503.description: Host power management is disabled in configuration

post.description: Minimum role: admin. Queues a task running the platform shutdown command (Windows shutdown /s, Unix shutdown -h). confirm:true is required. The agent needs the OS privilege the command itself demands — a refusal fails the task honestly. 503 when host_power.enabled is false.

grace_period.description: Seconds before the action fires (Unix rounds up to minutes)

message.description: Warning broadcast to logged-in users where the platform supports one

503.description: Host power management is disabled in configuration

post.description: Minimum role: admin. Same body rules as shutdown. 503 when host_power.enabled is false.

503.description: Host power management is disabled in configuration

post.description: Minimum role: admin. Manual intervention required to restart the machine. Same body rules as shutdown; Windows makes no shutdown/poweroff distinction. 503 when host_power.enabled is false.

503.description: Host power management is disabled in configuration

post.description: Minimum role: admin. No grace period, no graceful shutdown — the emergency stop. Requires BOTH confirm:true and emergency:true. 503 when host_power.enabled is false.

503.description: Host power management is disabled in configuration

get.description: Minimum role: viewer. Parses the platform hosts file (Windows System32\drivers\etc\hosts, /etc/hosts elsewhere) into structured entries plus the raw content.

put.description: Minimum role: operator. Provide structured entries or raw content (raw wins). A timestamped backup lands beside the file before the atomic replace. Writing needs the same OS privilege editing the file by hand would (Administrator on Windows, root on Unix) — a refusal fails honestly.

raw.description: Raw file content (takes precedence over entries)

500.description: Backup or write failure (typically missing OS privilege)

get.description: Minimum role: viewer. File sizes (main/WAL/SHM), table row counts, and SQLite internals for EVERY open database file — this agent splits storage by write-contention domain instead of one file.

post.description: Minimum role: admin. Rebuilds every open database file to reclaim space; temporarily doubles disk usage per file.
Minimum role: admin. Refreshes query-planner statistics on every open database file; lightweight and safe anytime.
Minimum role: admin. The same retention pass the periodic cleanup runs: finished tasks past tasks.retention_days plus stored telemetry past monitoring.retention_days.

get.description: Minimum role: admin. The whole store, plain — nothing masked (Mark's ruling: the user's local machine; the generated Hosts.yml carries these as SECRETS_* vars anyway).

put.description: Minimum role: admin. Replaces the submitted categories whole (the same top-level shallow-merge shape as PUT /settings); omitted categories are untouched. Rejected whole on an unknown category or an invalid entry name — the store never half-applies. Persisted atomically to secrets.yaml (0600) beside the config.

400.description: Unknown category or invalid entry name

get.description: Minimum role: admin. Returns the live configuration with the same section and key names as config.yaml.

put.description: Minimum role: admin. Shallow-merges the submitted top-level sections onto the on-disk YAML, validates the result, backs up the current file, and writes atomically. The running process keeps its loaded values — most changes apply on restart.

message.example: Settings updated successfully. Some changes may require a server restart.

500.description: Merged configuration invalid or write failure

get.description: Minimum role: admin. Section/field types, descriptions, defaults, ranges, and restart requirements — the document the settings UI renders its tabs from.

post.description: Minimum role: admin.

get.description: Minimum role: admin. Newest first.

delete.description: Minimum role: admin. Only agent-generated backup names (config-<unix-ms>.yaml) are accepted.

post.description: Minimum role: admin. The backup is validated as a working configuration and the current file is backed up first.

get.description: Minimum role: viewer. Whether ordered startup/shutdown is enabled (machines.orchestration.enabled) and the configured strategy. The base's SMF-controller handoff is illumos-only — this agent is the only lifecycle controller on its platform, so controller is hyperweaver-agent or none.

post.description: Minimum role: operator. Requires {"confirm": true}. Persists machines.orchestration.enabled to config.yaml and applies live: on the NEXT agent startup, autostart machines (zones.autostart in the spec) boot in settings.boot_priority order, highest first; agent exit with keep_running_on_exit false stops machines lowest-first.
Minimum role: operator. Persists machines.orchestration.enabled: false.

Minimum role: operator. Computes the SHUTDOWN execution plan over running machines — priority groups of ten, lowest first — without executing anything, plus a duration estimate (30s between groups + 120s per machine in the largest group, the base's arithmetic).

get.description: Minimum role: viewer. Every machine with its boot priority (settings.boot_priority, 1-100, default 95 — set via PUT /machines/{name} {boot_priority}, DB-immediate) plus the tens-range grouping.

post.description: Minimum role: operator. VBoxManage controlvm reset — the hard reboot beyond the stop→start chain (no guest shutdown; like pressing the reset button).
Minimum role: operator. VBoxManage controlvm pause — execution freezes in RAM (resume continues it; unlike suspend's save-to-disk).
Minimum role: operator. VBoxManage controlvm resume.

get.description: Minimum role: viewer (the machine-snapshots capability token). Synchronous — the live VirtualBox snapshot tree. node encodes the tree position (SnapshotName, SnapshotName-1, SnapshotName-1-1, ...); current marks the snapshot the machine's state derives from.

200.description: The snapshot tree (empty array when none)

404.description: Machine not found, or no VM exists behind it yet

post.description: Minimum role: operator. Queues snapshot_take (VBoxManage snapshot take). Works on running and stopped machines; live=true avoids pausing a running machine.
Minimum role: operator. Queues snapshot_restore — the machine reverts to the snapshot's state. VirtualBox only restores powered-off machines; the task fails with guidance while it runs.

delete.description: Minimum role: operator. Queues snapshot_delete — the snapshot's state merges into its children.

get.description: Minimum role: viewer. Synchronous VBoxManage guestproperty enumerate — the post-boot view: guest-additions data (/VirtualBox/GuestInfo/Net/* carries the guest's live IPs), OS info, and this agent's cloud-init keys.

404.description: Machine not found, or no VM exists behind it yet

get.description: Minimum role: operator. A short-lived (60s) UNBOUND ticket appended as ?ticket= to every WebSocket upgrade URL (task stream, SSH terminal, VNC websockify). Reusable within its lifetime; fetch a fresh one before each connect or reconnect.

WEBSOCKET upgrade — authenticate with ?ticket= (GET /ws-ticket), not API-key headers. Replays the task's buffered (or persisted) output as {type: "output", task_id, stream, data, timestamp} frames, streams live entries while the task runs, then sends {type: "status", task_id, status} and closes. Already-finished tasks get the full replay plus the status frame immediately.

post.description: Minimum role: operator. Mints an SSH terminal session for a RUNNING machine; connect the terminal at the /ssh/{sessionId} WebSocket. Each call is an independent session. Credentials come from the stored configuration (settings.vagrant_user/vagrant_user_pass/vagrant_user_private_key_path; the agent's provisioning key is the last-resort fallback). Transport = the pipeline's ladder: the provisioning NAT ssh port-forward first (immune to guest network reconfiguration), the document's control IP as fallback.

400.description: Machine not running, no credentials configured, or no SSH transport

get.description: Minimum role: viewer. Newest first. Sessions are in-memory — an agent restart closes them all (shells cannot survive it).
Minimum role: viewer.

delete.description: Minimum role: operator. Closes the shell and marks the session closed.

get.description: WEBSOCKET upgrade — authenticate with ?ticket= (GET /ws-ticket). Opens the interactive shell (xterm-256color PTY) for a session minted at POST /machines/{name}/ssh/start. Wire: remote output arrives as raw text frames; send raw text as terminal input; send {"type": "resize", "cols": N, "rows": N} JSON frames to resize the PTY. "Connecting to SSH..." opens the stream; "SSH connection closed." marks remote exit.

404.description: SSH session or machine not found

get.description: Minimum role: viewer. The machine's live VRDE console state — everything the UI needs before connecting: whether VRDE is on and its port, whether the host can actually speak VNC on it (vnc_capable: a usable VBoxVNC extpack module; without it the VRDE port speaks RDP, which noVNC cannot), and the websockify URL.

404.description: Machine not found, or no VM exists behind it yet

get.description: Minimum role: viewer (the machine-screenshot capability token). Synchronous PNG capture of the running machine's framebuffer (VBoxManage controlvm screenshotpng) — no console session or extpack needed.

502.description: Machine not running, or capture failed

get.description: WEBSOCKET upgrade — authenticate with ?ticket= (GET /ws-ticket). Bridges binary WebSocket frames onto the running machine's VRDE port (subprotocol "binary" — noVNC's wire). Requires an active VRDE console (settings.consoleport at create, or vnc: on via modify) AND a VNC-speaking VRDE module (the console list advertises vnc only then).

400.description: Machine not running, or no active VRDE console

get.description: Minimum role: viewer. One local template registry row (the base's GET /templates/local/{id}).

delete.description: Minimum role: operator. Queues template_delete: the disk image is released from VirtualBox's media registry and deleted, the version directory pruned, the row removed. Machines already built from it are untouched (they cloned their own media).

post.description: Minimum role: operator. Queues template_move: the disk image relocates to <target_path>/<org>/<box>/<version>/ (same-volume rename; cross-volume copy+delete) and the row's disk_path updates — the base's template move (zfs rename vs send-recv) in file terms.

target_path.description: The NEW storage root — the org/box/version layout is recreated beneath it

post.summary: Export a machine to a local .box
post.description: Minimum role: operator. Queues template_export (the machine must be powered off): VBoxManage export writes the machine as OVF + disk images, metadata.json marks provider virtualbox, and the tar.gz lands as a standard Vagrant box under <templates root>/exports/ — the path and sha256 in the task output. The base's zone → .box export in VirtualBox terms.

post.summary: Publish a template to a registry
post.description: Minimum role: operator. Queues template_upload (the base's publish): exports the machine (or takes an existing .box by path), ensures the registry structure (box → version → provider virtualbox → architecture; duplicates tolerated), chunk-uploads the artifact (100MB chunks, three retries with backoff), and releases the box. Registry credentials live on the configured source ONLY (auth_token, or username+api_key exchanged for a JWT at signin; ca_file trusts self-signed registries) — tokens never ride task metadata, so the base's per-request auth_token has no analog.

machine_name.description: Machine to export and publish (required unless box_path)

box_path.description: Existing .box file to publish (required unless machine_name)

post.description: Minimum role: admin. Under systemd the unit's Restart=always brings the agent back after a clean exit; everywhere else the agent spawns its own successor before shutting down.

message.example: Server restart initiated. Please wait a few seconds before reconnecting. The server will reload all configuration changes.
